domain: Bessemer converter (1856 steelmaking): pig iron 4-5%C in pear-vessel; air forced through bottom tuyeres oxidizes impurities in reactivity order Si→Mn→C; slag absorbs SiO2/MnO; CO carried out; refines to steel <1%C in 10-20 min.
specific_mechanism: Sequential bottom-up oxidation cascade driven by single forced air blast; impurities removed in thermodynamic reactivity order (Si most reactive first, then Mn, then C); externally forced 'noise stream' (air) drives intrinsically ordered cleanup.
llm_application: BESSEMER-BLAST forced-noise pretraining cascade: during pretraining, inject STRUCTURED noise tensor n_t at Layer-0 input on every step, drawn from n_t ~ N(0, σ_t^2 · Cov[V_phase]) where V_phase is the covariance matrix of TARGETED unwanted-feature directions for the current phase (Si phase = spurious surface-statistic features; Mn phase = syntactic over-fitting features; C phase = residual factual-overconfidence features). Schedule σ_Si > σ_Mn > σ_C in 3 staged decreasing-magnitude phases, each ~33% of training. After 3 stages σ → 0 and the network has been 'decarburized'. Differs from (a) noise injection for safety study (single Gaussian, not staged structured), (b) DNPO (preference noise during DPO, post-pretraining), (c) curriculum learning (data difficulty stages not noise targeting), (d) denoising autoencoder (input-corruption not targeted-feature directions) by combining (i) STRUCTURED targeted noise + (ii) REACTIVITY-ORDERED 3-stage schedule + (iii) FIXED LAYER-0 injection.
novelty_claim: Gaussian noise injection studies safety degradation. DNPO uses dynamic noise in DPO. Denoising autoencoders use random corruption. BESSEMER-BLAST recombines (a) STRUCTURED COVARIANCE-TARGETED noise + (b) 3-STAGE reactivity-ordered schedule + (c) FIXED LAYER-0 injection. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
content_words: sequential Si Mn C impurity removal

source_side: sequential Si Mn C impurity removal
